note: Hand-entered houston one-off events (curated holiday programming, etc.). Stable id, real lat/lon, ISO local startDateTime/endDateTime with the local offset. Past events are filtered automatically.

events.title: Freedom Over Texas 2026: Houston July 4th Fireworks at Eleanor Tinsley Park
events.description: Houston's official Fourth of July celebration, Freedom Over Texas, returns to Eleanor Tinsley Park along Buffalo Bayou on July 4, 2026 with a full day of live music, family-friendly activities, and a spectacular fireworks finale over downtown. The festival runs into the evening and caps the night with one of the city's biggest Independence Day fireworks shows. Kids 12 and under get in free.
events.cost: $15 per person; children 12 and under free

events.title: Sugar Land Red, White & Boom 2026: July 4th Fireworks at Constellation Field
events.description: Celebrate the Fourth of July at Constellation Field in Sugar Land with Red, White & Boom 2026, a family-friendly evening of live music, inflatables, face painting, food, and a Texas-sized fireworks display. The festival runs from 6 to 10 p.m. with fun for all ages, and kids 3 and under get in free.
events.cost: Tickets from $14.99; kids 3 and under free; limited free resident tickets

events.title: The Woodlands South County 4th of July Parade 2026: America 250 Celebration
events.description: The Woodlands' beloved 50th Annual South County 4th of July Parade marches through Market Street and The Woodlands Town Center on the morning of July 4, 2026, celebrating America's 250th birthday. Families line the route for floats, marching bands, a color guard, and patriotic fun. This free community parade is a cherished all-ages Fourth of July tradition.
events.venue: Market Street & The Woodlands Town Center

events.title: Star-Spangled Salute 2026: Free Houston Symphony Fireworks Concert in The Woodlands
events.description: Kick off Independence Day a night early at the free Star-Spangled Salute on July 3, 2026 at The Cynthia Woods Mitchell Pavilion in The Woodlands. The Houston Symphony performs patriotic favorites, with gates opening at 6:30 p.m. for family activities and photo ops with patriotic characters. The concert begins at 8:00 p.m. and no tickets are required for this all-ages tradition.

events.title: League City Fireworks Extravaganza 2026: Free July 3rd Fireworks & Festival
events.description: League City's free Fireworks Extravaganza lights up the sky at Chester L. Davis Sportsplex on July 3, 2026 from 6 to 10 p.m. Families can enjoy live music, inflatables, and food trucks before the big fireworks display begins at 9 p.m. Bring lawn chairs or a blanket for this free, all-ages Independence Day celebration.

events.title: Pasadena 4th Fest 2026: Free July 4th Festival & Fireworks at the Fairgrounds
events.description: Pasadena's 4th Fest returns to the Pasadena Municipal Fairgrounds on July 4, 2026 with free admission and free parking for the whole family. Enjoy live music, a car show, a Kids' Zone, food trucks, and amusement rides leading up to a spectacular fireworks finale at 9:15 p.m. It's one of the area's biggest free Independence Day festivals.

events.title: Baytown 4th of July Celebration 2026: Free Fireworks at Bicentennial Park
events.description: Baytown celebrates Independence Day at Bicentennial Park on July 4, 2026 with a free, family-friendly festival of live music, food vendors, kids' activities, and the city's signature fireworks show. Headliner Eli Young Band performs before fireworks light up the sky. This free, non-ticketed event marks America's 250th birthday for all ages.

events.title: Rosenberg Family 4th Celebration 2026: Free July 4th Fireworks at Seabourne Creek Park
events.description: Rosenberg's free Family 4th Celebration fills Seabourne Creek Nature Park with live music, food trucks, and family fun on July 4, 2026 from 6 to 10 p.m. The night ends with a spectacular 20-minute fireworks display at about 9:30 p.m. Free shuttle service makes this all-ages Independence Day festival easy for families to enjoy.
